title: nauclea vanderguchtii
intro: Nauclea vanderguchtii, also known as Quebra-Pé or African Dog Bone, is a tropical flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Nauclea vanderguchtii include treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as being used to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Nauclea vanderguchtii include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be made by boiling the roots or bark in water or using it in a topical cream.
